title: Battery Backup for CPAP in Apartment — 2026 Life-Saving Guide
description: CPAP users in apartments can't rely on gas generators. Here's how to choose the right battery backup to keep your therapy running all night during a power outage.
excerpt: If you depend on CPAP therapy, a power outage isn't just an inconvenience — it's a health risk. Here's how to keep your machine running all night in an apartment.
body:
Battery Backup for CPAP in Apartment — 2026 Life-Saving Guide

Updated May 2026 • 12 min read • Category: Guide

Why CPAP Users in Apartments Need a Plan

For the 30 million Americans who use CPAP therapy, a power outage isn't an inconvenience — it's a medical emergency. Without treatment, sleep apnea symptoms return immediately: interrupted breathing, oxygen desaturation, and increased cardiovascular strain.

Apartment dwellers face an extra challenge: gas generators are banned in most multi-unit buildings, and running one on a balcony can still let carbon monoxide seep into your unit — or your neighbor's. The CDC reports that CO poisoning from generators kills over 70 people annually, many in apartments.

A portable power station is the only safe, practical solution for apartment CPAP users. Here's what you need to know.

How Much Power Does a CPAP Machine Need?

CPAP ModelWatts (no humidifier)Watts (with humidifier)Nightly Wh (8 hrs, no hum.)Nightly Wh (8 hrs, with hum.)
ResMed AirSense 1030-53 W53-104 W~320 Wh~600 Wh
ResMed AirSense 1125-45 W45-90 W~280 Wh~540 Wh
Philips DreamStation 228-50 W50-95 W~300 Wh~560 Wh
Löwenstein Prisma 20A25-48 W48-88 W~290 Wh~520 Wh

Key takeaway: Running CPAP without the humidifier cuts power consumption by nearly half. If you can tolerate it, your battery backup will last twice as long.

Top Power Stations for CPAP in Apartments

1. Jackery Explorer 1000 v2 — Best for CPAP

Jackery Explorer 1000 v2

$999 · 1,070 Wh · 22 lbs · UPS mode (20ms)

This is our top pick for CPAP users. At 1,070 Wh, it runs a CPAP with humidifier for 2 full nights, or 3 nights without humidifier. The UPS mode switches in 20ms — your machine won't even blink during a power transition. At 22 lbs, it's easy to move from the bedroom to the living room.

2. EcoFlow River 2 Pro — Best Budget CPAP Backup

EcoFlow River 2 Pro

$549 · 768 Wh · 17 lbs · UPS mode (15ms)

If you only need one night of backup, the River 2 Pro delivers. It runs a CPAP without humidifier for 2 nights. At 17 lbs and $549, it's the most affordable entry point for CPAP users. The 15ms UPS switchover is the fastest in its class.

3. Bluetti AC180 — Best for Extended CPAP Use

Bluetti AC180

$899 · 1,152 Wh · 37 lbs

The AC180's 1,152 Wh capacity provides 2 nights with humidifier or 3-4 nights without. If you live in an area prone to multi-day outages (hurricane zones, wildfire PSPS areas), this is the safest bet.

CPAP Battery Backup Checklist

  • Check your machine's wattage: Look on the power brick or manual. Most CPAPs draw 30-60W without humidifier.
  • Skip the humidifier during outages: It doubles power draw. Use it only if medically necessary.
  • Use a DC converter if possible: Some CPAP models have a DC input that bypasses the AC-to-DC conversion loss, extending battery life by 15-20%.
  • Keep your machine plugged into the power station: Use UPS mode (if available) so it automatically switches during an outage.
  • Buy 1.5x your nightly needs: If your CPAP uses 300 Wh per night, get at least 450 Wh capacity. This gives you buffer for longer outages or higher pressure settings.

What Not to Do

  • Don't use a car inverter + extension cord setup. It's unsafe and inefficient. The inverter drains your car battery and CO from idling in a garage is deadly.
  • Don't rely on USB power banks. Most CPAP machines need pure sine wave AC power. USB adapters won't work.
  • Don't wait until an outage to test your setup. Test it on a normal night first — make sure the power station's fan noise doesn't disturb your sleep, and verify the runtime matches your expectations.

Final Verdict

Your CPAP therapy is non-negotiable. For most apartment CPAP users, the Jackery Explorer 1000 v2 is the optimal choice — it provides 2+ nights of backup, has instant UPS switchover, and is lightweight enough to move between rooms. If you're on a budget, the EcoFlow River 2 Pro covers one night with room to spare.
